paper_title: Bounded maximum degree conjecture holds precisely for $c$-crossing-critical graphs with $c \leq 12$
summary: The conjecture asks to determine the optimal values $D_c$ for each $c \in \{2, 3, \ldots, 12\}$; the source paper establishes $D_1 = 4$, $D_2 \geq 8$, and notes that $D_2$ should be within reach via the Bokal–Oporowski–Richter–Salazar characterisation of large 2-crossing-critical graphs, while the remaining $D_c$ are left to future work. A 2021 arXiv preprint (2112.04854) establishes order, maximum degree, and other parameters of large 2-crossing-critical graphs and may implicitly yield $D_2$, but its abstract does not state a specific value. No paper published strictly after 2021 that resolves any optimal $D_c$ value for $c \in \{2, \ldots, 12\}$ was found in a broad web search.
search_queries: crossing-critical graphs maximum degree bounds D_c optimal 2022 2023 2024
arxiv 1903.05363 Bokal Dvorak Hlineny crossing-critical bounded degree citations
crossing-critical graphs D_2 optimal degree bound crossing-critical maximum degree 2022 2023 2024 2025
notes: No follow-up paper from 2022–2026 resolving any optimal $D_c$ value was found. arXiv:2112.04854 ('Properties of Large 2-Crossing-Critical Graphs', 2021) establishes the maximum degree of large 2-crossing-critical graphs and may yield $D_2$, but its abstract does not confirm a specific numerical value; it predates the published year threshold so is not listed in since_posted. arXiv:2105.01104 ('On 13-Crossing-Critical Graphs with Arbitrarily Large Degrees', 2021) concerns $c \geq 13$ and is unrelated to the conjecture. The question of the exact optimal $D_c$ for $c \in \{2, \ldots, 12\}$ appears to remain open.
